title: Indonesia launches B50 biodiesel to curb fuel imports
summary:
Increased domestic demand for palm oil likely to reduce exports, analysts say

Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto officiates at the launch of the B50 biodiesel mandate in West Java on Thursday. All diesel sold in Indonesia is  now required to contain 50% palm oil.  © Reuters

fullArticle:
Increased domestic demand for palm oil likely to reduce exports, analysts say

Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto officiates at the launch of the B50 biodiesel mandate in West Java on Thursday. All diesel sold in Indonesia is  now required to contain 50% palm oil.  © Reuters

KARAWANG, West Java -- Indonesia has rolled out its B50 biodiesel mandate, increasing the required palm oil content from 40% to 50% in all domestically sold diesel to curb fuel imports and bolster energy security....
